channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: WestJet flight attendants face potential strike after failed negotiations
item.description: WestJet flight attendants are set to vote on a potential strike that could ground the airline's operations on August 2, 2026, if unresolved labor disputes persist. Over 4,400 unionized workers represe...

item.title: Edo State and US Investors Launch Electric Mobility Manufacturing Plant
item.description: Edo State has formed a strategic partnership with US-based MOSERI Holding Limited, JBV Manufacturing, and a tricycle company to establish an electric mobility plant for producing electric tricycles. T...

item.title: Keir Starmer bids farewell at his final Prime Minister’s Questions as MPs pay tribute before Andy Burnham succeeds him
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer made his final appearance at Prime Minister’s Questions before leaving office, marking the end of his political career with an emotional farewell in the House of Commons. During the s...

item.title: Iñigo Pascual Discusses Family Dynamics and Public Comparisons
item.description: The article highlights Iñigo Pascual's candid conversation regarding public comparisons between himself and his father, Piolo Pascual. Published on July 15, 2026, the piece explores how Iñigo navigate...

item.title: Supriya Sule Rejects NDA Switch, NCP (SP) Yet to Decide on Delimitation Bill
item.description: NCP (SP) leader Supriya Sule has dismissed speculation about her party supporting the Centre's proposed Delimitation Bill, stating that the party has not discussed the legislation with anyone nor been...

item.title: Mathira MP Eric Wamumbi Questions Gachagua's Political Stand after Meeting South Rift Delegation
item.description: Mathira MP Eric Wamumbi has criticised former deputy president and DCP leader Rigathi Gachagua after he hosted a delegation of opinion leaders from the South Rift at his Wamunyoro residence on Wednesd...

item.title: Property developer's five-year gambling debts exceed $20m, linked to betting platform incentives
item.description: Jean-Dominique Huynh, a failed property developer, has accumulated over $20 million in gambling losses across major betting platforms such as PointsBet, Sportsbet, Betr, and Entain. This figure surpas...

item.title: Erdogan seeks extradition of coup suspects as UK rejects Turkey's demands
item.description: Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan has renewed calls for the extradition of individuals he alleges were involved in a failed coup attempt against his government in 2016. The request, made during a...

item.title: Senator Durbin Questions Blanche Over Crypto Corruption Ties to Trump Family
item.description: During a Senate Judiciary Committee hearing, Senator Dick Durbin (D-IL) aggressively questioned Acting U.S. Attorney General Todd Blanche about his alleged ties to cryptocurrency corruption and wealth...

item.title: Shankar Das's Legal Battle: A Tale of Accusations and Reckoning
item.description: The excerpt from 'The Sea Has the Answers' by Shuma Raha follows Shankar Das, a former advertising executive, as he grapples with sudden accusations of sexual harassment. After being abruptly removed ...

item.title: CAD remains the most shorted major currency amid geopolitical tensions
item.description: The article discusses the Canadian dollar (loonie) being the most shorted major currency globally, according to a report by the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC). Scotiabank analyst Shaun Os...

item.title: Top 10 Most Expensive Shares on Nairobi Securities Exchange as Market Cap Reaches KSh 3.8 Trillion
item.description: The Nairobi Securities Exchange (NSE) has hit a record market capitalisation of KSh 3.88 trillion, driven by rising share prices and new listings like Kenya Pipeline Company and Family Bank. British A...

item.title: Napolcom vows to dismiss police officers involved in extortion as it investigates complaint over alleged illegal collections
item.description: The National Police Commission (Napolcom) has issued a strong warning against police officers involved in extortion, saying that anyone found collecting illegal payments or protection money, commonly ...

item.title: Fire damages three shops at Bara taxi rank, no injuries reported
item.description: A fire broke out at the Bara taxi rank in Soweto on Wednesday morning, damaging three shops and causing significant property loss. Property owner Sydney Morake confirmed the fire started inside a supe...

item.title: AJK home secretary condemns JAAC's social media campaigns and violent activities
item.description: Azad Jammu and Kashmir (AJK) Home Secretary Chaudhry Guftar Hussain has condemned the Joint Awami Action Committee (JAAC) for conducting coordinated campaigns on social media to incite people against ...

item.title: Oil Prices Rise Amid Iran's Threats to Disrupt Global Energy Supplies
item.description: Oil prices kept climbing for the fourth day in a row as tensions flared up in the Strait of Hormuz after the US-Iran ceasefire collapsed. Brent crude hit $85 per barrel, and WTI climbed past $80, driv...

item.title: Timothee Chalamet Joins Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders at World Cup Despite France's Defeat
item.description: Actor Timothee Chalamet, known for his role as 'Marty Supreme' in the film 'Baz Luhrmann's The Great Gatsby,' expressed his admiration for the Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders during the World Cup semifina...

item.title: Benue community mourns youth leader killed in gun attack
item.description: A youth leader, Sunday Ogoh, was murdered in a gun attack on the Ondo community in Ugboju district of Otukpo Local Government Area, Benue State. The attack, which happened early Tuesday, saw gunmen in...

item.title: Ebanel Urea Cream: A High-Concentration Solution for Treating Rough and Cracked Skin
item.description: The article highlights Ebanel Urea Cream, a highly concentrated urea-based product with 40% urea content, which is praised for its effectiveness in softening dead skin, reducing callus buildup, and re...

item.title: UK Condemns Iran's Involvement in European Attacks via Proxy Groups
item.description: The British Foreign Ministry has summoned Iran's top diplomat in London, Ali Nasimfar, accusing Tehran of directing proxy groups to carry out attacks across Europe between March and May. The Islamic R...
